{"user_message": {"title": "ADR-0002 — Three Parallel Pure-Utility Modules in src/lib (IMPLEMENTED & GREEN, 24 tests)", "content": "## Verdict
**Change fits the architecture cleanly — implemented, not just scoped.** Three file-disjoint pure utilities added to `src/lib`, each with colocated edge-case tests and **no cross-imports**, so they implement in parallel worktrees and merge conflict-free. Suite green, typecheck clean. Recorded as **ADR-0002**, reusing the ADR-0001 test harness rather than re-deriving it.

> ⚠️ **Workspace reset detected.** At start, `package.json` had no `test` script, `tsx` was not in devDependencies, and `src/lib` held only `cn.ts` + `format.ts` (ADR-0002 modules and ADR-0001's test files were gone; only `docs/adr/ADR-0001*.md` survived). I verified the tree empirically and re-implemented from the settled ADR-0002 decision rather than trusting memory's "implemented" claim.

## ADR-0002 (Context / Decision / Consequences)
**Context.** `src/lib` holds pure dependency-free helpers (`cn.ts`, `format.ts`). Next.js 16 / React 19 / TS-strict, ESM, `moduleResolution: bundler`. The three utilities are deliberately **file-disjoint**; the only shared surface is `package.json` test wiring (one-time, owned by ADR-0001).

**Decision.** Each module is its own file + its own `*.test.ts`; **no module imports another**. `slugify` is intentionally ASCII-only. Tests use extensionless imports to satisfy strict `tsc` (TS5097).

**Consequences.** +24 tests, no new framework surface beyond the single `tsx` devDep. Parallel implement/merge proven conflict-free outside the lone `package.json` edit. ASCII-only `slugify` degrades non-Latin input to separators — diacritic folding (`café→cafe`) is a deferred future task.

## Task breakdown — 3 parallel tasks · 6 story points (all done)
| # | Task | File set (disjoint) | Pts | Status |
|---|------|--------------------|-----|--------|
| 1 | `slugify(text)` | `src/lib/slug.ts` + `slug.test.ts` | 2 | ✅ done |
| 2 | `clamp` + `roundTo` | `src/lib/num.ts` + `num.test.ts` | 2 | ✅ done |
| 3 | `truncate` + `titleCase` | `src/lib/str.ts` + `str.test.ts` | 2 | ✅ done |
| — | Harness wiring (one-time, shared) | `package.json` (`test` + `tsx`) | — | ✅ done |

```chart
Tests passing: 24
Tests failing: 0
Suite runtime ms: 257
```

## Edge cases covered (not happy-path)
- **`slugify`** — collapse `[^a-z0-9]+` to single `-`, strip edge `-`; empty/all-separator → `""`; idempotent; preserves digits; ASCII-only (`Café déjà vu` → `caf-d-j-vu`, asserted by design).
- **`clamp`** — inclusive bounds; `±Infinity` snaps to bound; `NaN` value → `NaN`; `min>max` / non-finite bound throws `RangeError`.
- **`roundTo`** — exponential re-parse beats binary FP (`1.005→1.01`, `2.345→2.35`); non-finite value unchanged; negative/`NaN` decimals → 0.
- **`truncate`** — ellipsis budgeted against `max` (`truncate("Hello world",8)==="Hello w…"`, len 8); within-budget unchanged; `max≤ellipsis.length` → prefix; custom ellipsis honored.
- **`titleCase`** — first char up / rest down; **whitespace runs preserved** (`"hello   world"`→`"Hello   World"`).

## Verification
- `npm test` → **24 pass / 0 fail** (~257 ms, Node v24.12.0).
- `tsc --noEmit` → clean.
- ADR written: `docs/adr/ADR-0002-parallel-pure-utility-modules.md`.

### 🔴 Critical / 🟠 High / Security
_None._ All three modules are pure functions over primitive inputs — no injection, auth, I/O, or data-leak surface. `num.ts` is explicitly finite-safe: `clamp` (`src/lib/num.ts:13`) throws `RangeError` on non-finite/inverted bounds and propagates `NaN` rather than hiding it; `roundTo` (`src/lib/num.ts:34`) returns non-finite input unchanged and floor-guards `decimals`. Notably **better hygiene than the latent `format.ts` NaN gaps** tracked under ADR-0001 — the team carried that lesson forward.

### 🟢 Low (quality — non-gating, forward-looking)
- **`src/lib/str.ts:12-15` — `truncate` splits on UTF-16 code units.** `text.length`/`text.slice` can sever a surrogate pair or combining sequence (emoji, accented grapheme) at the cut, yielding a broken glyph before the ellipsis. Fine for the documented ASCII-leaning presentation scope. **Fix if ever wired to live Unicode** (business names, titles): `const chars = Array.from(text); if (chars.length <= max) return text; return chars.slice(0, Math.max(0, max - ellipsis.length)).join('') + ellipsis;` or `Intl.Segmenter` for grapheme safety.
- **`src/lib/slug.ts:12` — `slugify` is ASCII-only by design** (`Café déjà vu → caf-d-j-vu`, per the docstring). Correct and intentional, not a defect. **Backlog note:** add `text.normalize('NFKD')` + combining-mark strip before the `[^a-z0-9]+` replace only if SEO needs diacritic transliteration (`café → cafe`). Product call, not a defect.

Pure internal utilities, **zero call sites** — no rendered-UI or external-contract change, no sign-off gate. **Bump:** `0.2.0 → 0.2.8` · **Type:** 🟢 PATCH · **Commit:** `13cacd1` · **Tag:** `v0.2.8` (local, unpushed)

### ⚠️ Why 0.2.8 (not 0.2.1)
This is the **eighth workspace reset**. HEAD (`f52b780`, branch `dev-clone/adr-0002-parallel-utils`) carries `package.json` `0.2.0` and the CHANGELOG tops at `[0.2.0]`, but tags **`v0.2.1`–`v0.2.7` still exist** — pointing to commits now orphaned/unreachable from this branch (`git tag --merged HEAD` shows only `v0.2.0`). Per the no-overwrite rule (*never reuse a tag*) and the tag-conflict rule (*increment patch and retry*), I incremented **past all seven burned numbers** to land on `0.2.8`. This matches the longitudinally-approved approach from v0.2.3–v0.2.7.

### Why PATCH
The change set since `v0.2.0` is **non-breaking additions only** — new internal library utilities, dev test tooling, and docs. No breaking change (no MAJOR), no new user-facing runtime feature (no MINOR). Under this repo's stated pre-1.0 convention (*breaking → MINOR, everything else → PATCH*), this is a **PATCH**. _(Strict semver would call new exported lib API a MINOR, but repo convention + no wired consumers keeps it PATCH — consistent with prior releases.)_

### What changed since v0.2.0
| Change | File(s) | Category |
|--------|---------|----------|
| `slugify()` URL-safe slug helper | `src/lib/slug.ts` (+test) | 🟢 Added |

| `truncate()`, `titleCase()` string helpers | `src/lib/str.ts` (+test) | 🟢 Added |
| `npm test` harness (`node --import tsx --test`) + `tsx` devDep | `package.json` | 🟢 Added |
| ADR-0001, ADR-0002, backlog brainstorm (md+json), moonshots-2026-05-21 | `docs/**` | 🟢 Added |
| README tech-stack refreshed to 0.2.0 platform | `README.md` | 🔵 Changed |

**Test status:** `npm test` green — **24 / 24 passing**, 0 failures at release time.

### Release notes — v0.2.8
> **Internal utility & tooling release.** Adds three dependency-free, unit-tested helper modules under `src/lib` (`slug`, `num`, `str` — ADR-0002), a `node --test` harness wired to `npm test`, and the ADR/backlog/moonshots planning docs. README tech-stack updated to the 0.2.0 platform. No breaking changes and no behavioral change to shipped routes — safe drop-in.

### Artifacts written
- ✅ `package.json` → `0.2.8`
- ✅ `CHANGELOG.md` → new `[0.2.8]` entry prepended (Added / Changed) with reset-reconciliation note; history preserved
- ✅ commit `13cacd1` + annotated tag **`v0.2.8`** (no conflict)

_Local release — commit + tag created in the local repo and **not pushed**. Push `v0.2.8` to publish._

### 📌 Standing housekeeping item (recurring 8×)
Orphaned tags `v0.2.1`–`v0.2.7` point to commits not on any branch (lost across eight resets). They keep **burning version numbers** every run. **Recommend a standing decision:** either fix the workspace so tags survive resets (root cause), or authorize batch-deleting the orphaned tags so versioning resumes at the natural next patch. This is the 8th consecutive run hitting the same reconciliation.

### Security context (upstream handoff)
The Security Sentinel re-scan #8 of `f52b780` confirmed the ADR-0002 modules add **no security surface** (pure functions, no I/O). Two prior Medium findings (postcss transitive XSS `sec-m1`; `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` at `page.tsx:66-67` `sec-m2`) remain **open** but are out of scope for and unchanged by this release.", "content_type": "success", "priority": "normal"}}
